articles.headline: 12 Weeks of Intermittent Fasting Keeps Weight Off a Full Year Later
articles.body: A new study shows that 16:8 time-restricted eating—where you squeeze all your eating into an eight-hour window—maintains weight loss long-term. Researchers found that overweight and obese adults who did the diet for just 12 weeks combined with Mediterranean eating principles lost more weight than those with a typical eating schedule, and most kept it off a full year later. The best part: early and late eating windows worked about equally well, giving you flexibility to pick what fits your life.
articles.takeaway: Short bursts of structured eating can reset your weight for lasting results. You don't need to overhaul forever—12 weeks can create a year of change.
articles.pexelsQuery: intermittent fasting meal prep clock schedule

articles.headline: Probiotics Beat Expectations at Stopping Acid Reflux
articles.body: Forget thinking probiotics are just for digestion. New research shows that adding probiotic therapy on top of standard GERD treatment actually solves the root problem. The study found that probiotics reshape your gut microbiome in ways that keep reflux symptoms away, not just mask them. Patients got real relief that stuck around—their microbiota composition actually shifted to one that prevents reflux from returning. It's the difference between turning off the symptom and fixing the mechanism.
articles.takeaway: If antacids are your routine, probiotics might be the missing piece. They work by rebuilding your gut's natural defense, not by suppressing acid.
articles.source: Nucleic Acids Research / Inner Mongolia Agricultural University

articles.headline: Your Afternoon Coffee Actually Changes Your Brain at Night
articles.body: Here's something coffee addicts don't expect: caffeine consumed hours before bed shifts your brain's wiring during sleep itself. New research on brain activity shows caffeine increases neural complexity and pushes your brain toward a more excitable state, especially during deep sleep. Younger adults (20-27) felt this effect more during REM sleep, but the pattern held across ages. It's not just that coffee keeps you wired—it literally alters how your brain fires and recovers while you're unconscious.
articles.takeaway: Caffeine doesn't just stay in your system; it restructures your brain's activity pattern during sleep. Cut it off by 2 PM if deep recovery matters to you.
articles.pexelsQuery: brain sleep neural activity rest recovery

articles.topic: aerobic exercise knee osteoarthritis pain management
articles.headline: Walking and Cycling Beat Everything Else for Knee Pain
articles.body: A massive review of over 200 studies settles the debate: aerobic exercise—walking, cycling, swimming—outperforms every other type of training for knee arthritis pain and mobility. Researchers analyzed data on thousands of participants and found aerobic activity showed the strongest evidence for both short and long-term relief. Other exercises help, but none matched the consistent gains from steady cardio. All forms of exercise were safe, but experts now recommend aerobic training as the foundation of any knee arthritis treatment plan.
articles.takeaway: You don't need fancy equipment or high intensity. Steady aerobic work—especially the kind you'll actually stick with—wins every time for joint pain.
articles.pexelsQuery: walking cycling swimming exercise outdoor activity

quote.text: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
quote.author: James Clear
quote.context: Bestselling author of Atomic Habits and expert on habit formation and continuous improvement.

workout.title: 5K Pace Track Ladder: 400m Repeat Pyramid
workout.tip: Aim for even splits on each 400m repeat—don't chase the first one. Your quads will feel the fatigue on repeats 4–6; use shorter, quicker strides to maintain cadence rather than grinding with long strides.
workout.warmup: 8 min: 2 min easy jog, 6×100m stride-outs with 50m walk recovery between each

exercises.name: 6×400m @ 5K Pace
exercises.notes: Run each 400m at your 5K goal pace (e.g., if 5K goal is 20 min, that's ~6:25/km or 10:20/mile = ~90 sec per 400m). Focus on controlled acceleration in the first 100m, hold steady in the middle, kick slightly in the final 100m.

exercises.name: 90-Second Recovery Jog
exercises.notes: Between each 400m repeat, jog at conversational pace (about 65–70% max heart rate). This active recovery clears lactate and keeps your heart rate elevated for the next effort.

workout.cooldown: 4 min: easy jog at conversational pace, followed by 3 min of standing quad stretch (30 sec each leg), hamstring fold (60 sec), and calf stretch against a wall (30 sec each side)
workout.description: A classic pyramid structure that builds speed sustainability at 5K race pace. Athletes run 400m repeats at progressively harder efforts (6×400m with 90-second recovery jogs), teaching the body to maintain turnover under fatigue. Perfect for 5K and 10K runners who need to sharpen leg speed before race season.

meal.title: Whole Grain Toast with Whipped Ricotta, Figs & Pistachios
meal.description: A Mediterranean-inspired open-faced toast that pairs slow-digesting whole grains with creamy ricotta and natural carbs from figs—perfect pre-endurance fuel that won't spike and crash your blood sugar. The healthy fats from pistachios help with satiety and nutrient absorption during long efforts.
ingredients: 2 slices whole grain or sourdough bread
meal.instructions:
1. Toast bread to your preferred crispness—about 2–3 minutes.
2. While bread toasts, stir ricotta with lemon zest and a pinch of salt until smooth and airy.
3. Spread whipped ricotta generously on each toast.
4. Top with fresh figs (halved) or chopped dried figs.
5. Drizzle with honey and scatter pistachios over top.
6. Serve immediately.
meal.tip: Make whipped ricotta the night before and store in an airtight container—spreads even easier and saves 3 minutes on weekday mornings. Dried figs work year-round and are more stable for meal prep.
